events.text: Patient is a 46-year-old Black female with a BMI of 30.1. No prior medical history. Paternal grandmother had breast cancer. BRCA1/2 negative. CHEK2 variant detected.

At age 44, patient presented with bloody nipple discharge from the left breast. Workup revealed invasive ductal carcinoma (8500/3), left breast upper inner quadrant (C50.2), grade 3, ER negative, PR negative, HER2 IHC 3+, Ki-67 72%.
At age 44, left breast core biopsy: invasive ductal carcinoma with micropapillary features, Nottingham grade 3. ER negative (
At age 44, mammogram showed a 2.8 cm mass in the left upper inner quadrant with extensive associated microcalcifications spanning 5 cm, BIRADS 5.
At age 44, MRI breast showed a 3.0 cm enhancing mass with extensive non-mass enhancement over 6 cm, suggesting extensive DCIS. Suspicious left axillary lymph node.
At age 44, PET/CT staging showed left breast mass (SUVmax 8.9), left axillary node (SUVmax 5.4), and no distant metastatic disease.
At age 44, stage IIB (cT2N1M0) left breast invasive ductal carcinoma, HER2-positive, ER/PR-negative with extensive DCIS.
At age 44, received neoadjuvant TCHP for 6 cycles.
At age 44, baseline LVEF 64%. During TCHP: grade 2 alopecia, grade 2 nausea, grade 1 fatigue, grade 2 neutropenia managed with pegfilgrastim.
At age 45, underwent left total mastectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy. Pathologic complete response (pCR): ypT0 ypN0. No residual invasive or in situ carcinoma. 0 of 4 sentinel nodes positive.

At age 45, left mastectomy: pathologic complete response (pCR). Fibrosis and chronic inflammation at tumor bed. No residual invasive carcinoma or DCIS. All 4 sentinel nodes negative. Treatment effect consistent with complete response.
At age 45, given pCR, continued adjuvant trastuzumab and pertuzumab to complete 1 year of HER2-directed therapy (per APHINITY approach).
At age 45, adjuvant chest wall radiation 50 Gy in 25 fractions given initial node-positive disease (cN1).
At age 45, LVEF 60% at mid-adjuvant therapy. Stable. No cardiac symptoms.
At age 45, completing adjuvant HP therapy. Tolerating well. ECOG 0. Discussed reconstruction options. Surveillance plan established.
At age 45, surveillance CT at 6 months post-mastectomy showed no evidence of disease.
At age 46, patient developed new right-sided weakness. MRI brain showed a solitary 2.2 cm left parietal lobe enhancing lesion consistent with brain metastasis.
At age 46, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at time of brain recurrence showed small bilateral pulmonary nodules (largest 8 mm) suspicious for metastatic disease.
At age 46, recurrent metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer with brain and lung metastases. Recurrence within 1 year of completing adjuvant therapy.
At age 46, underwent stereotactic radiosurgery to the left parietal brain metastasis, 21 Gy single fraction.
At age 46, initiated trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) 5.4 mg/kg every 21 days given brain metastasis and recent prior trastuzumab exposure.
At age 46, on T-DXd: grade 2 nausea managed with antiemetics, grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 decreased appetite. Baseline CT chest clear for ILD monitoring.
At age 44, tumor profiling (FoundationOne CDx): ERBB2 amplification (copy number 20), TP53 R248Q, CHEK2 germline pathogenic variant (c.1100delC), ARID1A truncating mutation. TMB 8 mutations/Mb. MSS.

At age 46, on T-DXd with good tolerance. Right-sided weakness improving after SRS. ECOG 1. Close MRI brain surveillance every 3 months. Monitoring for ILD symptoms and imaging. Discussed next-line options including tucatinib-based combinations if needed.
At age 46, MRI brain 2 months post-SRS showed decreasing left parietal lesion with surrounding edema resolving. CT chest showed stable pulmonary nodules.

scenario_blurb: Metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer. IHC 3+ or FISH amplified. Treated with trastuzumab-based regimens (THP, T-DM1, trastuzumab deruxtecan). Include hormone receptor status variation (ER+/HER2+ and ER-/HER2+). Some with brain metastases.
